projectDescription: A TypeScript-based N-body gravitational physics simulation with real-time HTML5 Canvas rendering.
keyFeatures: N-body gravitational physics simulation using Newton's law of universal gravitation
Shell theorem implementation for accurate gravity inside massive bodies
Real-time rendering with HTML5 Canvas and performance monitoring
Interactive controls for adding celestial bodies and manipulating orbits
Vector-based physics with velocity, acceleration, and force calculations
Support for static and dynamic bodies with configurable mass and radius
publicApiSurface: Body class - Represents a celestial body with position, velocity, mass, and physics calculations
Body.constructor(config: BodyConfig) - Creates a new body with configuration
Body.update(dt: number, bodies: Body[]) - Updates body physics for the current frame
Body.calculateAllForces(bodies: Body[]) - Calculates net gravitational forces from all other bodies
Body.calculateShellGravityForce(body2: Body) - Calculates gravity force using shell theorem
Body.applyAcceleration(acceleration: Vector2) - Applies acceleration to the body
Body.resolveElasticCollision(body1: Body, body2: Body) - Handles elastic collision between bodies
TinyCanvas class - Lightweight HTML5 Canvas wrapper for rendering
TinyCanvas.loop(callback: (dt: number) => void) - Starts animation loop with delta time
TinyCanvas.circle(x: number, y: number, r: number, color?: string) - Draws a filled circle
TinyCanvas.drawVector(origin: Vector2, vector: Vector2, color?: string, scale?: number) - Draws a vector arrow
TinyCanvas.text(txt: string, x: number, y: number, size?: number, color?: string) - Renders text
Vector2 type - 2D vector with x and y components
BodyConfig type - Configuration object for creating bodies
